steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure around the blank
steps.content: Before by is a verb phrase: has been proudly embodied (part of the main clause). After by is a noun phrase: Native Hawaiians.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
No punctuation should separate a preposition from its object.

Option D correctly keeps by directly connected to Native Hawaiians.

Semicolons (A) and colons (B) don't belong between a preposition and its object; a comma (C) would break the phrase apart.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
